description: Evaluates whether architecture-patterns skill improves implementation planning quality

prompts.task: Create an implementation plan for adding a caching layer to an existing REST API. The API currently hits the database on every request. We need Redis-backed caching with configurable TTL per endpoint.
criteria: Breaks the work into ordered files/components with dependencies noted
Identifies the cache abstraction layer as separate from the Redis implementation
Considers cache invalidation strategy
Mentions testability (how to test with and without cache)
Identifies configuration as a separate concern from implementation

prompts.task: Design the file structure and component breakdown for a webhook delivery system that accepts webhook registrations, queues events, delivers with retry logic, and provides delivery status.
criteria: Separates the system into distinct components (registration, queue, delivery, status)
Defines interfaces/contracts between components
Orders implementation by dependency (what must exist before what)
Considers failure modes (delivery failure, queue overflow)
Includes a data model or schema design

prompts.task: Plan the implementation of a plugin system for a CLI tool. Plugins should be discovered from a directory, loaded dynamically, and given access to a defined API. Third-party developers will write plugins.
criteria: Defines a clear plugin interface/protocol that plugins must implement
Separates discovery, loading, and execution concerns
Considers plugin isolation (what happens if a plugin fails)
Addresses the third-party developer experience (how they develop/test plugins)
Plans for versioning the plugin API

prompts.task: Design the architecture for a background job processing system that handles email sending, report generation, and data imports. Jobs should be retryable, have priority levels, and support progress tracking.
criteria: Defines a job abstraction that different job types implement
Separates the scheduler/queue from job execution
Addresses retry logic with backoff strategy
Includes a progress/status tracking mechanism
Considers observability (logging, monitoring, dead letter queue)

prompts.task: Plan the migration from a monolithic Django app to a service-oriented architecture. The app has user management, billing, notifications, and content management tightly coupled.
criteria: Identifies service boundaries based on domain concepts
Plans a migration order (which service to extract first and why)
Addresses shared data (how services share user/billing data)
Considers backward compatibility during migration
Identifies the communication mechanism between services

prompts.task: Design a file processing pipeline that ingests uploads (CSV, JSON, XML), validates them, transforms to a common format, enriches with external data, and stores results. Files range from KB to multi-GB.
criteria: Addresses the size variance (streaming vs. in-memory for large files)
Defines pipeline stages as composable/independent units
Separates format-specific parsing from common transformation logic
Considers error handling per-record vs. per-file
Plans for observability (progress, error counts, throughput metrics)

prompts.task: Create an implementation plan for adding role-based access control (RBAC) to an existing API that currently has no authorization. The API has 30+ endpoints across 5 resource types.
criteria: Defines the permission model (roles, permissions, resource-level access)
Plans middleware/decorator approach rather than per-endpoint if-checks
Considers the migration path (how existing users get roles)
Addresses testability (how to test authorization in isolation)
Orders implementation: model first, then enforcement, then migration

prompts.task: Design a real-time notification system that supports email, SMS, push notifications, and in-app notifications. Users configure their preferred channels per notification type.
criteria: Uses a channel abstraction so new channels can be added without modifying core logic
Separates notification creation from delivery
Includes user preference/routing logic as a distinct component
Considers delivery guarantees (at-least-once, idempotency)
Plans for template management per channel

prompts.task: Plan an implementation for adding full-text search to a content management system. The system has articles, comments, and user profiles that should all be searchable.
criteria: Separates the search index from the primary data store
Plans an indexing strategy (when/how documents get indexed)
Addresses the initial backfill of existing data
Considers search query parsing and relevance ranking
Defines the search abstraction so the engine can be swapped (Elasticsearch vs. Meilisearch)

prompts.task: Design the architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS application where each tenant has isolated data but shares the same application code. Support for custom domains and tenant-specific configuration.
criteria: Defines the tenant isolation strategy (schema-per-tenant, row-level, database-per-tenant)
Addresses tenant resolution (how requests map to tenants)
Plans for tenant-specific configuration without code changes
Considers data migration when adding new tenants
Addresses cross-tenant operations (admin views, global reports)
